title: Luwak DICOM Anonymizer Configuration
description: Configuration schema for the Luwak DICOM anonymization pipeline.

inputFolder.description: Source directory or file for DICOM files to be anonymized.

outputDeidentifiedFolder.description: Destination directory for anonymized DICOM files.

outputPrivateMappingFolder.description: Destination directory for UID mappings and metadata exports.

recipes.description: List or single string of recipe names to apply. Can be built-in or custom recipe files.

projectHashRoot.description: Salt for deterministic UID generation and date shifting.

patientIdPrefix.description: Prefix for sequential patient IDs (e.g., 'Zenta' generates 'Zenta00', 'Zenta01', etc.).

maxDateShiftDays.description: Maximum number of days for date shifting (default 1095).

excludedTagsFromParquet.description: List of DICOM tags to exclude from Parquet export. Accepts integer or string formats (e.g., 2145386512, '7FE0,0010', '(7FE0,0010)').

logLevel.description: Logging level for the anonymization process.

analysisCacheFolder.description:
Path to folder for analysis cache databases (patient_uid.db and llm_cache.db).
If specified and folder exists with databases, they will be loaded and updated; if not, new databases will be created.
Databases persist across anonymization runs to ensure consistent mappings.
If not specified, temporary databases are created in the private mapping folder and deleted after processing.

physicalFacePixelationSizeMm.description: Target block size in millimeters for face pixelation during defacing. Controls the resolution of pixelation blocks, with larger values providing stronger anonymization. Default 8.5mm works well across different resolutions.

selectedModalities.description: List of DICOM modalities to include in processing. If empty or not set, all modalities are included.

keepTempFiles.description: If true, temporary directories created during processing (temp_organized_input, temp_defaced_organized) are retained after the workflow completes. Useful for step-by-step validation of the deidentification pipeline.

testOptions.description: Options for testing, including mask defacer paths.

useExistingMaskDefacer.description: List of file paths for mask defacer.

customTags.description: Paths to manually revised tag CSV files for standard and private tags.

standard.description: Path to the manually revised standard tags CSV file.

private.description: Path to the manually revised private tags CSV file.

bypassCleanDescriptorsLlm.description: If true, bypasses the LLM call in clean_descriptors_with_llm. The result is always treated as 0 (no PHI detected) and the tag is kept unchanged.

saveDefaceMasks.description:
Configuration for persisting the primary defacing mask per (patient, study, FrameOfReferenceUID, modality) group.
When the 'primary' list is non-empty, luwak selects one series per group – the one with the largest spatial coverage and finest voxel resolution – runs ML defacing on it, and stores the resulting mask as an NRRD file in the private mapping folder.
Subsequent series in the same group reuse the cached mask, avoiding redundant ML inference.
The study UID is included in the cache key so masks are scoped to a single study and are not shared across studies.
The mask database follows the same persistence rules as analysisCacheFolder: if that folder is configured the database persists across runs; otherwise it is temporary.
When this option is absent or 'primary' is empty, the feature is fully disabled: no DB is created, no masks are saved, and all series are defaced independently.

primary.description: List of DICOM modalities for which the primary defacing mask should be cached and reused (e.g. ["CT", "MR"]). An empty list or absent key disables the feature.
